description: §21.8.1 — a `pinned` modifier on the import of a `const` (`import { appPhase pinned }` where m.scrml exports `const appPhase`) does NOT fire E-IMPORT-PINNED-INVALID. Per the best-effort Option A policy, const/let pins are ACCEPTED because an engine-form export (`export <engine var=appPhase>` desugaring to `export const appPhase`) is indistinguishable from an arbitrary const today.
rationale: §21.8.1: `pinned` is meaningful for cell/engine-typed names; engines desugar to `export const`, so a pinned const is a possible engine pin and is accepted (best-effort — trades false negatives for zero false positives). E-IMPORT-PINNED-INVALID must not fire on a const pin — the clean twin of the function-pin case.
